title: qa_execution_completed event payload
$comment: Provenance: authored 2026-04-23 in WU 3.3 as the QA execution success signal. Pairs with qa_execution_failed.schema.json. Idempotence key is (task_correlation_id, commit_sha): the qa-execution skill refuses to emit a second event with the same pair. Downstream consumers (qa-regression in WU 3.4, qa-curation in WU 3.5) key off this event to confirm a plan has been exercised against a specific commit without regression.
description: Payload shape for the `qa_execution_completed` event emitted by the QA agent on an all-pass qa-execution run. The event's envelope carries a feature-level correlation_id; the payload's `task_correlation_id` narrows to the specific qa_execution task that produced the signal, and `commit_sha` pins the commit the plan was run against.

task_correlation_id.description: Task-level correlation ID of the qa_execution task that produced this event. Together with `commit_sha`, forms the idempotence key.

commit_sha.description: Git commit SHA of the assigned repo's `main` HEAD at execution time. For cross-repo features, each qa_execution task carries its own `commit_sha` (the SHA of the repo it is assigned to). 40-char full SHA; short SHAs are rejected to keep the idempotence key unambiguous across history.

plan_path.description: Path to the test plan file that was executed, relative to the product specs repo root. Architecture §4.3 fixes `/product/test-plans/` as the canonical location.

test_count.description: Number of tests in the plan's `tests` array at execution time. On success all of them passed; the count is duplicated from the plan for event-log self-containment so a consumer reading the event log without pulling the plan file can reason about coverage breadth.

total_duration_seconds.description: Wall-clock seconds for the full per-test loop. Optional — a run whose duration cannot be measured (e.g., retried after a crash before the skill captured the end timestamp) may omit the field rather than fabricate a value.
